hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 8, J. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
Flop — 3, Queen, Queen. Your opponent bet. You called.
River — 3. Your opponent fired a large river bet.

wrongReasons: 7 5 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 7 5 would have.
Q K makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
6 J is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 6 J would have.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 10

Your opponent flopped two pair and bet all three streets with growing sizing. The consistent pressure — especially the larger turn bet — signals a hand strong enough to protect but not quite a monster.
